name: error-handling-fundamentals
description: Auto-invoke when reviewing try/catch blocks, API error responses, async operations, or user feedback patterns. Enforces graceful degradation and meaningful error messages.
capabilities: Reviews try/catch blocks for proper error handling
Identifies anti-patterns like empty catch blocks
Provides retry logic and fallback strategies
Validates error logging and debugging practices
author: DanielPodolsky
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing error handling guidelines and best practices. All 39 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. SKILL.md is markdown documentation with code examples - the backticks flagged are markdown syntax, not shell execution. skill-report.json is pure metadata. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or external commands exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Poor error handling leads to silent failures and user frustration. This skill teaches you to write resilient code that gracefully handles failures and provides clear feedback to users.
limitations: Focuses on JavaScript and TypeScript error patterns

useCases.title: Learn Error Handling Patterns
useCases.description: Understand proper try/catch usage, error boundaries, and recovery strategies through guided code reviews.

useCases.title: Standardize Error Practices
useCases.description: Ensure consistent error handling across your team codebase with automated review checklists.

useCases.title: Handle API Failures Gracefully
useCases.description: Review frontend and backend error handling to prevent crashes and improve user experience.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Error Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this error handling code and check if it follows best practices for user experience and debugging.

promptTemplates.title: API Error Handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this API error handling code for proper status code checks, retry logic, and user feedback.

promptTemplates.title: Async Error Patterns
promptTemplates.scenario: Reviewing Promise and async/await error handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Check this async code for proper error propagation, catch blocks, and error context preservation.

promptTemplates.title: Error Boundary Review
promptTemplates.scenario: Reviewing React error boundaries or similar patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Evaluate this error boundary implementation for proper error catching, logging, and fallback UI rendering.

bestPractices: Always provide meaningful error messages that users can understand without technical jargon
Include error context for debugging while hiding sensitive details from users
Use finally blocks for cleanup operations like resetting loading states
antiPatterns: Empty catch blocks that silently swallow errors without any handling
Exposing stack traces or technical error details to end users
Catching errors too early and returning null without context for callers

faq.question: Which programming languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: Currently focuses on JavaScript and TypeScript patterns with examples for React and Node.js environments.

faq.question: Will this skill fix my errors automatically?
faq.answer: No, it provides review feedback and suggestions. You implement the fixes based on the recommendations.

faq.question: Can I use this for production code reviews?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill follows industry best practices and includes security considerations for production applications.

faq.question: How does this handle async/await versus Promise chains?
faq.answer: The skill reviews both patterns and provides specific guidance for each error propagation approach.

faq.question: What if my error handling is framework-specific?
faq.answer: Principles apply universally with examples for React, Express, and other common framework patterns.

faq.question: Does this cover logging and monitoring?
faq.answer: Yes, it includes guidelines for proper error logging, severity levels, and what information to capture.
